ip_outcome_note: Card name is Zygarde; PokemonTCG.io confirms nationalPokedexNumbers: [718]; flavor text and oracle text ('Core Avenger') reference Zygarde's cell-based biology canonically. Art depicts 50% Forme (serpentine, glowing green core), matching the confirmed in-game sprite. Internal Pokemon IP — no external crossover risk.

trivia_bullets.text: Vivid Voltage (set code VIV / swsh4) is the fourth expansion in the Sword & Shield TCG series, released November 13, 2020 in English. Its marquee card is Pikachu VMAX (no. 044/185), tying the set's theme to Pikachu's Gigantamax form from Pokemon Sword & Shield. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4 set metadata]

Zygarde is National Pokedex no. 718, introduced in Generation VI (Pokemon X & Y, 2013). It is the third member of the Aura Trio alongside Xerneas (Life) and Yveltal (Death), collectively representing the Norse Yggdrasil guardians: Xerneas the deer (soil), Yveltal the bird (upper tree), and Zygarde the serpent (underground). [Bulbapedia: Aura trio, via WebSearch snippet]
sources: Bulbapedia: Aura trio, via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Zygarde has three known formes: 10% Forme (dog-like, ~10% of cells gathered), 50% Forme (serpentine, depicted on this card), and Complete Forme (activated when HP drops below half with Power Construct ability — more powerful than either Xerneas or Yveltal). The card depicts 50% Forme. [Bulbapedia: Zygarde, via WebSearch snippet]
"Born when all of Zygarde's cells have been gathered together, it uses force to neutralize those who harm the ecosystem." [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-93]

Core Avenger, Zygarde's signature attack on this card, does 80 base damage + 80 bonus damage if the player has more Prize cards remaining than the opponent — a come-from-behind mechanic thematically aligned with Zygarde's lore role as the force that restores balance when the ecosystem is thrown off-order. [PokemonTCG.io: swsh4-93 oracle text]
